_readme: This file controls what AI agents can do on your Synology NAS. Read tools are safe — they only look at things and run automatically. Write tools change state and ALWAYS ask for your approval before doing anything. To remove a tool, delete its name from the enabled list. To add a write tool, copy its name from _write_tools_available into enabled_write_tools. Push to GitHub for changes to take effect.

_tool_descriptions.check_system_info: DSM version, NAS model, uptime, CPU, memory — baseline context for any conversation
_tool_descriptions.list_volumes: Discovers active NAS data volumes and their filesystem usage so later tools do not assume everything lives on /volume1
_tool_descriptions.list_shared_folders: Lists DSM shared folders with their real paths and selected metadata
_tool_descriptions.inspect_mounts: Shows the live mount graph for volumes, package storage, and bind mounts
_tool_descriptions.inspect_encryption_state: Checks whether volumes and share paths appear mounted, unlocked, and visible to packages
_tool_descriptions.check_disk_space: Shows disk and inode usage for all active data volumes
_tool_descriptions.check_hardware_temps: CPU/chassis temperature sensors, fan speeds, and SMART disk temperatures
_tool_descriptions.check_volume_health: DSM-layer RAID/volume health (synovolumestatus, synoarraystatus), mdstat, SMART overall
_tool_descriptions.check_cpu_iowait: Measures how much time the CPU is stuck waiting on disk — high values mean an I/O problem
_tool_descriptions.check_agent_container: Confirms the Synology Monitor agent container is running
_tool_descriptions.get_resource_snapshot: Full picture: top processes, disk activity, network connections, memory, and recent errors — best starting point for any problem
_tool_descriptions.check_io_stalls: Finds processes stuck waiting on disk, measures disk queue depth, and looks for hung task warnings in the kernel
_tool_descriptions.check_memory_detail: Full meminfo, swap activity rates, dirty/writeback pages, OOM kill history
_tool_descriptions.check_network_health: Interface errors/drops, routing table, DNS resolution, listening ports, NFS/SMB connections
_tool_descriptions.check_tailscale: Tailscale VPN status — interface state, IP, and whether the daemon is reachable
_tool_descriptions.check_network_connections: Active TCP connections per process, connection state counts, top peers, Drive sync connections
_tool_descriptions.check_security_log: Failed logins, security events, admin audit entries, SSH connections
_tool_descriptions.check_active_sessions: Currently active SMB, NFS, SSH, DSM web, and Synology Drive sessions
_tool_descriptions.check_packages: All installed DSM packages, their running status, and recent package events
_tool_descriptions.tail_system_log: Recent /var/log/messages — general kernel and service events
_tool_descriptions.tail_drive_server_log: Shows the most recent Synology Drive server log entries
_tool_descriptions.search_drive_server_log: Searches Drive server logs for a specific word, share name, or error
_tool_descriptions.tail_sharesync_log: Shows the most recent ShareSync log entries across all volumes — what files are being synced and any errors
_tool_descriptions.check_sharesync_status: Looks specifically for stuck, conflicted, or erroring ShareSync tasks across all volumes
_tool_descriptions.check_kernel_io_errors: Reads the kernel error log for disk errors, SCSI faults, and filesystem corruption
_tool_descriptions.check_share_database: Lists all shared folders from the DSM database — failures indicate database corruption
_tool_descriptions.check_drive_package_health: Checks whether the Synology Drive package is properly installed across all volumes
_tool_descriptions.check_drive_database: Checks Synology Drive's internal databases for corruption across all volumes
_tool_descriptions.search_webapi_log: Searches DSM WebAPI logs for share access errors and authentication failures
_tool_descriptions.search_all_logs: Searches every log file on the NAS for a specific word or phrase
_tool_descriptions.find_problematic_files: Finds files with names that break ShareSync — special characters, conflict files, and extremely long names
_tool_descriptions.check_filesystem_health: Checks filesystem mount status, inode usage, RAID status, and SMART disk health across all volumes
_tool_descriptions.check_scheduled_tasks: Lists all DSM scheduled tasks and their last run result — catches silent cron failures
_tool_descriptions.check_backup_status: Checks Hyper Backup status and recent backup log entries across all volumes
_tool_descriptions.check_container_io: Shows which Docker containers are doing the most disk I/O
_tool_descriptions.check_package_runtime: PHASE-1B: Checks a specific package's runtime state — synopkg status, enabled flag, PID files, lock files, matching processes
_tool_descriptions.check_daemon_processes: PHASE-1B: Shows process state for key Synology daemons: synologand, invoked, syncd, cloud-control, syno_drive_server
_tool_descriptions.inspect_package_lockfiles: PHASE-1B: Finds stale lock files across all packages and runtime dirs that may prevent package starts
_tool_descriptions.inspect_crash_signals: PHASE-1B: Looks for crash evidence — segfaults, OOM kills, core dumps, DSM error log events
_tool_descriptions.tail_package_logs: PHASE-1B: Shows recent log entries for a named DSM package from all known log locations
_tool_descriptions.search_package_logs: PHASE-1B: Searches all log files for a named DSM package and a filter term
_tool_descriptions.inspect_path_metadata: PHASE-1E: Shows POSIX metadata for an exact path — owner, group, mode, size, inode, timestamps
_tool_descriptions.inspect_path_acl: PHASE-1E: Shows POSIX and Synology ACL entries for an exact path via getfacl and synoacltool
_tool_descriptions.inspect_effective_permissions: PHASE-1E: Shows effective access on a path with optional per-user group membership and share-level access check
_tool_descriptions.run_command: Run any read-only command the AI needs for deep diagnosis — blocked automatically if it tries to write anything
_tool_descriptions.restart_monitor_agent: WRITE: Restarts the Synology Monitor agent container — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.stop_monitor_agent: WRITE: Stops the Synology Monitor agent — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.start_monitor_agent: WRITE: Starts the Synology Monitor agent — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.pull_monitor_agent: WRITE: Downloads the latest monitor agent version — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.build_monitor_agent: WRITE: Rebuilds the monitor agent container — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.restart_nas_api: WRITE: Restarts the NAS API container — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.restart_synology_drive_server: WRITE: Restarts the Synology Drive package — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.restart_synology_drive_sharesync: WRITE: Restarts the ShareSync package — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.restart_hyper_backup: WRITE: Restarts the Hyper Backup package — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.rename_file_to_old: WRITE: Renames a problem file by adding .old — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.remove_invalid_chars: WRITE: Removes sync-breaking characters from a filename — always asks your approval first
_tool_descriptions.trigger_sharesync_resync: WRITE: Forces a ShareSync re-sync by restarting ShareSync — always asks your approval first
